conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
So you got in safely and on time. What's been the most challenging part of your week?
Honestly, just getting out of bed in the morning has been a struggle. I mean, my alarm clock is like, 10 feet away from me, but somehow it feels like an insurmountable hurdle.
I understand. It sounds like sleep has been a challenge for you lately. Can you tell me more about that?
Yeah, I just can't seem to get a good night's sleep anymore. And when I do, I'm always exhausted the next day.
That must be frustrating. How has your work as a plumber been affected by this fatigue?
To be honest, it's been pretty tough. I've had to take on extra shifts just to keep up with my workload, but even that feels like too much some days.
It sounds like you're feeling overwhelmed. Have there been any changes in your social life or relationships lately?
Not really, no. Just the usual stuff. My friends and family are all good, I think. But work has just been consuming me lately.
Got it. So work is taking up a lot of your mental energy. How about other things you enjoy? Do you have any hobbies or interests that bring you joy?
Um, not really. I used to love playing guitar, but now it's just too much effort. And my favorite TV show has been collecting dust on my DVR for weeks.
That sounds like you're struggling to find pleasure in activities. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or eating habits?
Yeah, sometimes I'll skip meals because I don't have the energy to cook or order takeout. And when I do eat, it's just stuff that's easy and quick.
Okay, so you're experiencing some difficulties with eating and sleep. How about your energy levels? Do you feel like you can focus on tasks without getting tired easily?
Honestly, no. I get mentally foggy after an hour or two of work, and it takes me forever to recover from a long day.
It sounds like you're struggling with concentration too. Have there been any recent stressful events in your life that might be contributing to these challenges?
Well, my boss has been breathing down my neck about meeting certain deadlines, and I just feel like I'm drowning in paperwork sometimes.
Okay, so work is a significant source of stress for you right now. Who do you turn to when you're feeling overwhelmed or need support?
Um, mainly myself, I guess. My friends are all busy with their own stuff, and my family doesn't always understand what's going on.
Okay, so it sounds like you're relying on yourself for support right now. What are some things that you're proud of accomplishing lately?
Hmm... well, I did finish that one big project at work last week, which was a relief.
That's something to build on! What are your goals for our time together today? Is there anything specific you'd like to accomplish or work through?
Honestly, just figuring out how to get some sleep and maybe regaining my energy would be amazing. And not feeling so guilty all the time would be great too.
Okay, let's start working on those goals then! What do you think is the first step in getting your sleep back on track?
I'm not sure... I just don't know where to start sometimes.
That's okay. We can explore some strategies together. Can you tell me more about this guilt you're feeling?
It's just stuff that happened at work, and now I feel like I've let everyone down. And it's hard for me to forgive myself sometimes.
Okay, so there are some negative thoughts patterns going on around work-related events. Have you noticed any physical sensations or body feelings when you're experiencing this guilt?
Yeah, my stomach gets all knotted up and I start sweating.
Okay, let's explore that further. Can you tell me more about what happens physically when you feel guilty?
Well, like I said, my stomach hurts and I get hot flashes sometimes. And sometimes I just feel really numb inside.
Okay, so there are some physical sensations accompanying your guilt. What do you think is the first step in addressing these feelings of guilt?
I don't know... maybe just talking about it more would help?
That's a great start! Let's keep exploring that further. How do you feel like our conversation has gone so far?
Honestly, it feels kind of like a weight is being lifted off my shoulders already.
Okay, that's a great sign! What are your goals for the next step in our process together?
I guess just figuring out how to make some changes and moving forward would be amazing.
